name: Windows Network Access Suspicious desktop.ini Action
notes:
Converted from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Tim Shelton (HAWK.IO)
SIGMA Title: Windows Network Access Suspicious desktop.ini Action
SIGMA Description: Detects unusual processes accessing desktop.ini remotely over network share, which can be leveraged to alter how Explorer displays a folder's content (i.e. renaming files) without changing them on disk.

False positives:
- Read only access list authority

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event matches devicetype=12 AND "Event ID"=5145 AND "Object Type"='File' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%\desktop.ini%' AND (LOWER("Rule Name") LIKE '%writedata%' OR LOWER("Rule Name") LIKE '%delete%' OR LOWER("Rule Name") LIKE '%writedac%' OR LOWER("Rule Name") LIKE '%appenddata%' OR LOWER("Rule Name") LIKE '%addsubdirectory%') AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Windows Network Access Suspicious desktop.ini Action on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when the event matches devicetype=12 AND "Event ID"=5145 AND "Object Type"='File' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%\desktop.ini%' AND (LOWER("Rule Name") LIKE '%writedata%' OR LOWER("Rule Name") LIKE '%delete%' OR LOWER("Rule Name") LIKE '%writedac%' OR LOWER("Rule Name") LIKE '%appenddata%' OR LOWER("Rule Name") LIKE '%addsubdirectory%') AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Windows Network Access Suspicious desktop.ini Action
